reviews.review_msg: Nice guy,not actually that bad, probably improved from all the bad comments he's gotten. Got rid of the textbook, but has this reading list now filled with articles. His exams though were pure memorization of pretty much word for word from any of the slides. Especially with terrible questions like match the author with their book/article.

exams are based on insignificant facts.  If this were a course on the periodic table, he would quiz you on the atomic mass to 5dp for two elements rather than ask about differences between metals and metalloids.  WASTE OF MONEY AND TIME.  I can't believe this is a requirement for arch students.  Took FNST101 and it was much more informative.

the worst prof ever!! lecture class is super boring. Dont even use the textbook.So hard on the exams. Basically u have to memorize individual things based on the notes. If u wannt try to get an  "f" , definitely go for him. All i can say is dont waste ur money and time.

Spent the last half of the semester talking about how bad natives have it...Gave us a slide show in the abosulte darkness So we couldn't take notes. We asked him if it would be on the exam because we couldn't see and he said no. Come exam time low and behold it was the essay question....avoid!

This was a terrible class. The exams tested your ability to memorize, and not learn concepts. He asks for dates, specific names, and sites. An essay question on the midterm was on a slideshow he gave us with the LIGHTS OFF - how the heck were we supposed to take notes with the lights completely off?! Class averages on tests were low - around 60%.

He's a good prof, it's just his teaching style is very difficult for students to process. It's true he wants insignificant details when testing so just studying everything and you'll be fine, there isn't that much information. 2 pages typed of notes per lecture.

I was very interested in the class, before it started. He teaches all over the place, favouring native views and opinons. His exams were had ridiculous questions that appeared out of no where (there were even questions about stuff he said wouldn't be on it). The lectures that the TA did where 110x better then his.
Hard exams, stupid questions. Almost completely avoided the material that we were supposed to be learning and favoured films/info about Native rights.

Worst class/prof ever. Made boring material worse, and the marking scheme was totally random. Likely exam question: What is the name, birthdate, and driver's license number of the archaeologist who excavated this site? From at student with a 3.8 GPA (would have been higher if not for him).
HARD MARKER. ack. study everything!!!!! dont go to class hung over.

He was a super nice person but his exams were ridiculously hard. He had us memorize every minute detail of his lectures and films. No critical thinking in this class. His lectures were a bit dry too, and the textbook didn't correlate to his lectures well.
The most rediculous test questions ever...don't leave class when he shows a film...he'll ask you wasteful questions on the next quiz!  BLECH
Nice guy. Bad teacher. Test questions are rediculous. He is not interested in testing what you learned but how well you memorize.
testes inane trivia that has nothing to do with course material....asked who author of an article was. A lot of material, wants you to memorize all of it.

Nice guy,not actually that bad, probably improved from all the bad comments he's gotten. Got rid of the textbook, but has this reading list now filled with articles. Awesome prof! Was always upbeat and made jokes that stuck in your head. There were three quizzes, one midterm, and a paper, and that was it. TA was extremely helpful (he practically told us what was on the quizzes and midterms). =)

This man is awesome!!!!!  He totally made a class I wan't too excited about totally interesting and fun!  He is always upbeat and funny, and makes you want to listen!  This is a great prof, such a nice change from the bulk of SFU's Arch dept.  Rock On Ross Jamieson!
fun guy, like being taight by Jim Henson
Great lecturer, enthusiastic, uses powerpoint!

Awesome prof! Was always upbeat and made jokes that stuck in your head. There were three quizzes, one midterm, and a paper, and that was it. TA was extremely helpful (he practically told us what was on the quizzes and midterms). =)

Great lecturer, enthusiastic, uses powerpoint!

fun guy, like being taight by Jim Henson
Great lecturer, enthusiastic, uses powerpoint!
Not so good prof. talks really fast. would help if his lectures are recorded. Marks very hard.. NOT recommended..

Lecture slides are PACKED. It's distracting during lecture but really helpful for review later. Lectures also taped. Great lectures, great insight. My grade was lousy but the entire grade was tests and I don't test well, I'm better at written assignments. If you test well this class is good for you. Good texts and supplementary materials.

Lectures are long, however if you don't want to go, he posts recordings and slides on canvas! Tests are fairly straight forward, but there is a lot to study. Probably wouldn't take again, but he isn't a terrible prof.

Dr. Maxwell well if my major ain't archaeology, I wouldn't take his lecture, period. Super long slides (average 80 pages per slide) I understand there is a lot to cover, but the sequence is very confusing. I have to go back and forth to re-organize my notes before the exam. Want good grade? Study for it, I mean STUDY STUDY STUDY.  A LOT OF NOTES!

Dr Reimer was an excellent professor. He is very passionate about what he teaches and does really care about his students and plans workloads accordingly. While he is slightly disorganized and forgets to upload discussion posts, the workload is very manageable if you don't procrastinate. If youre looking for an interesting Arc course, I reccomend!

Rudy is an amazing person, but he is also extremely chaotic and disorganized. He cancels lectures, forgets to put in all the assignments to canvas (like midterms), and as a result, you are missing the grade. However, he is very caring as well. With a good TA it all might work, but his lectures still need more structure.

Rudy, his lectures and the content and course work are great, but he cancels class often, sometimes less than an hour before class. He sometimes neglects to add marks into canvas, so your grade ends up lower because your assignments may be 'missing' (this is bad when there are only 3 assignments) and he is IMPOSSIBLE to get ahold of.

There was a LOT reading. Probably could have got away with not doing some of it, but the assigned papers were relevant. Lecture was a bit disorganized, but interesting overall. Tutorial got cancelled a lot, which wasn't anyone's fault but it was frustrating. I'd recommend to anyone interested in archaeology or in Indigenous history.

One of my absolute favourite professors in the Archaeology program. He cares about local archaeology and introduces his class to some of the current field work. He provides large amounts of interesting First Nations background information. Always willing to talk during after class or during office hours, 100% would take again.

Dr. Rudy is a great guy and lectures are really informative and interesting. It's too bad the class wasn't better organized, communication was lacking and expectations for assignments weren't at all clear (even before the class moved online). The midterm questions were so confusing. Do your readings and know the content really well for exams.

Rudy is one of the absolute best lecturers I've ever had. He's so engaging and so passionate about this course content. If you need to take arch273 take it with Rudy! The exams and paper are pretty straightforward and the grading is very fair

RUDY IS AWESOME!!!! he is so nice and his course material is interesting and very organized. he puts a lot of effort into his lectures and material. this is a prof who genuinely cares and makes school worthwhile
